prompt: Compiler error: cannot return reference to temporary value in Parser::next_token

turns.content:
<task>
Getting compiler error: "cannot return reference to temporary value" in Parser::next_token. Help me fix it.
</task>

error[E0515]: cannot return reference to temporary value
  --> src/parser.rs:21:9
   |
19 |         let token = &self.input[start..self.pos];
   |                      ---------- temporary value created here
21 |         token
   |         ^^^^^ returns a reference to data owned by the current function

arguments.reasoning: Actually wait - the error message says 'temporary value' but self.input is owned by the struct. Let me look more carefully. The issue is that `self.input[start..self.pos]` borrows self.input, then on line 20, self.pos is mutated (self.pos += 1), which means there's a mutable borrow of self while the immutable borrow (token) is still alive. The borrow checker can't allow both. The fix: either return an owned String, or compute the slice indices and return the slice after the mutation. Let me restructure to do the pos increment before creating the borrow.

arguments.message: The issue is a borrow conflict. On line 19, `token` borrows `self.input` immutably. On line 20, `self.pos += 1` borrows `self` mutably. Both borrows are alive at return. The fix is to save the end position, do the mutation, then create and return the slice in one step.

arguments.title: Rust borrow conflict: mutation before return reference
arguments.content: When returning &str from struct field, any mutation of self between creating the reference and returning it causes borrow conflict. Fix: complete all mutations first, then create and return the reference in the last statement.

turns.content: Finding recorded: Rust borrow conflict: mutation before return reference

arguments.summary: Fixed borrow conflict by saving end position, performing self.pos mutation, then creating the slice reference last. Also added bounds clamping for safety. Compiles cleanly.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed the borrow conflict. The issue was mutating self.pos (line 20) while an immutable borrow of self.input (token) was still alive. Restructured to save end position, mutate self.pos, then create and return the slice reference.
